sections.paragraph:
κόπριον, ου, τό (s. prec. and two next entries; Heraclitus 96; Epict. 2, 4, 5; Plut., Pomp. 48, 2; OGI 483, 81 [II b.c.]; PFay 110, 5; 10; POxy 502, 32 καθαρὰ ἀπὸ κοπρίων; Jer 32:33; Sir 22:2)

① dung, manure κόπρια βάλλειν put manure on Lk 13:8 (Theophr., Caus. Pl. 3, 9, 5 παραβάλλειν κόπρον).

② filth, dirt, transf. sense of 1 (BGU I, 15, 50 [I b.c.]) τὰ κ. αἴρειν (PGM 4, 1441) take away the dirt Hs 9, 10, 3.—DELG s.v. κόπρος. M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Heraclitus Heraclitus , phil., V b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

transf. transf. = transferred

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 559
